user.title: Connect to Copilot Bridge
user.description: Connect Home Assistant to the Copilot bridge service. Enter the bridge URL and optional API key, then submit to validate the bridge and continue to MCP settings.

hassio_confirm.title: Connect to discovered Copilot Bridge add-on
hassio_confirm.description: Home Assistant discovered the {addon} add-on at {url}. Enter the bridge API key if configured, then submit to continue.

zeroconf_confirm.title: Connect to discovered Copilot Bridge
zeroconf_confirm.description: Home Assistant discovered {name} at {url}. Enter the bridge API key if required, then submit to continue.

github_config.title: Configure GitHub access
github_config.description: Choose how you want to initialize GitHub access for the bridge. The recommended flow is browser sign-in: open the GitHub page, enter the code, and approve access. Current bridge status: {current_status} Browser sign-in status: {browser_signin_status}. Configured bridge token: {configured_token_status}. Auth storage: {auth_storage_status}.

github_device_flow_options.title: Start GitHub sign-in
github_device_flow_options.description: Choose the GitHub OAuth scopes to request before starting browser-based sign-in.

manual_token.title: Set GitHub token
manual_token.description: Provide a GitHub token to validate and store on the bridge.

github_device_flow.title: Authorize GitHub device flow
github_device_flow.description: Open {verification_uri} in a browser, enter code {user_code}, and approve access. Requested scopes: {scope}. After authorizing, submit again to continue. Current status: {status_message}

mcp_config.title: Configure Home Assistant MCP
mcp_config.description: Configure MCP after GitHub setup. This bridge is designed to pair GitHub Copilot with Home Assistant context through MCP. Enable this when you want Copilot prompts to use your Home Assistant MCP server.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the bridge service
error.invalid_url: Enter a full bridge URL such as http://copilot-bridge:8099
error.invalid_auth: Failed to validate the GitHub token
error.device_flow_error: GitHub device flow could not be completed
error.device_flow_not_available: GitHub browser sign-in is not available on the bridge
error.bridge_auth_not_configured: GitHub auth is not configured on the bridge yet
error.clear_auth_failed: Failed to clear GitHub auth on the bridge

init.title: Manage GitHub access
init.description: Manage GitHub access through the Copilot Bridge. The recommended flow is browser sign-in: open the GitHub page, enter the code, and approve access. Current bridge status: {current_status} Browser sign-in status: {browser_signin_status}. Configured bridge token: {configured_token_status}. Auth storage: {auth_storage_status}.

github_device_flow_options.title: Start GitHub sign-in
github_device_flow_options.description: Choose the GitHub OAuth scopes to request before starting browser-based sign-in.

manual_token.title: Set GitHub token
manual_token.description: Provide a GitHub token to validate and store on the bridge.

github_device_flow.title: Authorize GitHub device flow
github_device_flow.description: Open {verification_uri} in a browser, enter code {user_code}, and approve access. Requested scopes: {scope}. After authorizing, submit again to continue. Current status: {status_message}

mcp_config.title: Configure Home Assistant MCP
mcp_config.description: Configure MCP after GitHub setup. This bridge is designed to pair GitHub Copilot with Home Assistant context through MCP. Enable this when you want Copilot prompts to use your Home Assistant MCP server.

error.invalid_auth: Failed to validate the GitHub token
error.device_flow_error: GitHub device flow could not be completed
error.device_flow_not_available: GitHub browser sign-in is not available on the bridge
error.bridge_auth_not_configured: GitHub auth is not configured on the bridge yet
error.clear_auth_failed: Failed to clear GitHub auth on the bridge
